title: romulea columnae subsp. occidentalis
intro: Romulea columnae subsp. occidentalis is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae, commonly known as a type of wild iris, native to southern Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, derived from its roots and leaves. The health benefits of Romulea columnae subsp. occidentalis include treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as being used as an antiseptic and antispasmodic. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the roots and leaves in water or oil, and can be used topically or taken internally in moderation, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically studied.
